Identity.Name: StingStatusCodes
Identity.Definition: Phase status codes that describe an element's lifecycle state — existing, new, demolished, temporary, future, relocated, retained. Maps to Revit phase data and to IFC IfcOwnerHistory.State plus phase-filter constructs. Used by STING's PhaseAutoDetect and by drawing-type phase routing.

Governance.StandardsBasis: STING PhaseAutoDetect taxonomy; IFC4 IfcOwnerHistory.State; UK BS 1192 phase conventions

Value.Definition: Existing — present before this project; remains.
New — installed by this project. Default for newly placed elements.
Demolished — removed by this project. Element retained in the model for demolition phase but excluded from new / handover phases.
Temporary — installed for the construction phase only; removed before handover (hoarding, temp lighting, site cabin, sequencing prop).
Future — provisioned in this project for a later phase. Capped pipework, marked-out plant area, future-fit cable container.
Relocated — existing element moved to a new position during this project. Carry through demolition + new phases together.
Retained — existing element explicitly preserved (heritage / structural retention) and protected during works.
Unknown / not yet assigned. PhaseAutoDetect should populate this; XX at Stage 4+ indicates a missed automation.
